message:
feat(fnp-random): bit-exact shuffle/permutation/choice infrastructure for RandomState

Add four methods on fnp_random::RandomState that mirror numpy's legacy
rk_interval-based Fisher-Yates exactly:

  shuffle_slice<T>(&mut [T])          — in-place Fisher-Yates
  permutation_f64(&[f64]) -> Vec<f64> — cloned-and-shuffled
  permutation_range(usize) -> Vec<i64> — shuffle of 0..n
  choice_indices_unweighted(n, size)  — replace=true, p=None fast path

All four draw `random_interval(i)` per iteration matching the C-level
mtrand routine, so a future native fast-path replacement of the current
PyRandomState passthrough (random_state_numpy_legacy_method) can drop
in without breaking bit-exact parity.

Also: regenerate fnp_python/RANDOM_COMPLIANCE.generated.md. After the
concurrent PyRandomState port of the 15 missing distributions via the
legacy passthrough helper (binomial/poisson/multinomial/dirichlet/
multivariate_normal/negative_binomial/noncentral_chisquare/noncentral_f/
hypergeometric/logseries/vonmises/wald/choice/shuffle/permutation), the
compliance matrix now reports 56/56 MUST = 100%, 3/3 SHOULD = 100%,
2/2 MAY = 100% — closing the acceptance bar for duat.

Absorbed from concurrent agent work on this file: one `fn logistic`
formatting collapse, a `v *= v * v` → `v = v * v * v` clippy cleanup
in standard_gamma (algebraically identical), and ULP-trimming of four
lognormal oracle literals in the test fixture.

Closes franken_numpy-duat.

br-franken_numpy-duat
